title: ricinocarpus boivinianus
intro: Ricinocarpus boivinianus is a plant species, also known as the Boivin's stinkwood, which is native to Madagascar and parts of Africa. The plant's bark and roots are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits associated with Ricinocarpus boivinianus include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are typically made by boiling the bark and roots in water to extract its medicinal compounds.
